title: Cloud Firestore

sections.markdown: Cloud Firestore is a serverless, real-time, globally-distributed NoSQL document database. Data lives in **collections** of **documents** (max 1 MiB); queries return snapshots or Streams; access is enforced by declarative Security Rules.

rows: Batch related writes; avoid write hot spots

sections.markdown: Firestore charges per document read — that shapes design. **Denormalize** aggressively (embed hot fields, keep counts on parent docs) and paginate with cursors, not offsets. A poorly designed screen can be 100× more expensive than a well-designed one.
Sequential inserts to a monotonically-increasing doc ID (like a timestamp) → **hotspot** on one server range → 500ms tail latencies. Add high-cardinality prefix or shard the ID.

Use Security Rules like `allow read: if request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId`. Composite indexes get auto-suggested — accept them. Transactions and batched writes are up to 500 ops each. Use `FieldValue.arrayUnion` / atomic increments for concurrent updates.

flashcards.back: Collections of documents (≤1 MiB each); documents can contain subcollections.
Reads, writes, deletes, egress, and storage — reads dominate for most apps.

flashcards.front: How to avoid a write hotspot on doc IDs?
flashcards.back: Add high-cardinality prefixes or shard the key so writes spread across ranges.

quiz.explain: Documents live in collections; documents can host subcollections.
Rules run server-side on every request.
Every read costs — cache and paginate.

challenges.text: CRUD a users collection with the Flutter SDK.
Add a real-time snapshot query with a composite index.
Enforce ownership via Security Rules + custom claims for role.
Refactor a hot write path to eliminate a monotonic-key hotspot.

interviewQuestions: Firestore data model and cost model?
